title: Clary sage and bergamot soy jar candle
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle with a herbal-floral mid note and a bright citrus accent
excerpt: Clary sage essential oil has a complex herbal character with a slight floral sweetness; bergamot adds a bright, citrus-forward accent that lifts the blend. Together they give a fresh and slightly sophisticated scent. A CD-12 wick in a 200 ml jar gives a 25 to 30-hour burn.
sourceNotes: Soy wax container candle technique: Candlescience wick sizing guide (2023); CD-12 for 6 to 7 cm jar diameter. Fragrance load at 8% by wax weight: maximum for GW 464 soy wax. Clary sage (Salvia sclarea) EO: herbal, slightly floral mid note. Bergamot EO (Citrus bergamia, cold-pressed peel): phototoxic on skin but safe in a room candle. FCF (furocoumarin-free) bergamot can be used if skin contact is a concern in room spray applications, but is not relevant here.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (130 g soy wax fill)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale weights by the number of jars. One pre-tabbed wick per jar.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for 3 to 5 days before burning for the best fragrance throw.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Wax stable for 5 years or more. Bergamot is a top note and may fade slightly faster than clary sage; the blend should remain recognisable for 2 years. Keep the lid on between burns.

glossaryTerms.definition: The scent given off by an unlit candle. Citrus-forward top notes like bergamot tend to perform well on the cold throw because they volatilise readily at room temperature.
The temperature at which liquid wax is poured into the container. Soy container wax is poured at 55 to 65°C; too hot and the wax pulls away from the sides; too cool and it sets unevenly.

content.text: Clary sage has a herbal, slightly floral character that differs noticeably from the culinary sage in the kitchen; it is softer and more complex, with a light muskiness. Bergamot brings the recognisable citrus brightness of Earl Grey tea peel and lifts the heavier herbal note of the clary sage. Bergamot gives a good
because it volatilises readily at room temperature; clary sage comes through more on the hot throw during the burn. Bergamot essential oil is phototoxic on skin but poses no risk in a candle.

items.prepNote: Container-blend soy wax; GW 464 or equivalent
4.2% of wax weight; herbal, floral mid note
3.8% of wax weight; citrus top note; phototoxic on skin but safe in a candle
CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm diameter jar

content.text: Place the soy wax in the candle pouring pitcher and set in a pan of barely simmering water. Heat, stirring occasionally, until fully melted and at 80°C. Remove from the heat and allow to cool.
Secure the sustainer tab of the pre-tabbed wick to the centre of the jar base. Thread the wick through the centring bar and lay the bar across the jar rim so the wick hangs plumb in the centre.
Add the essential oils and pour
When the wax has cooled to 60°C, add the clary sage and bergamot essential oils and stir for 2 minutes. Pour at
: between 55 and 65°C. Pour in a slow, steady stream down the side of the jar, leaving a 5 to 10 mm gap at the top. Leave undisturbed until fully set.
Leave for at least 1 day before burning; 3 to 5 days gives the best fragrance throw. Trim the wick to 5 mm before each burn.

Keep the lid on between burns. Wax stable for 5 years or more; fragrance should remain recognisable for about 2 years, though bergamot as a top note may fade slightly faster than the clary sage. Store in a cool, dark place.
